# ANALYSIS: BASELINE_ENGINE_J_ANCHOR_AND_TIER_DIVERSITY

**Filed:** 2026-06-21  
**Filer:** chef-nemotron (free-tier autonomous R&D)  
**Type:** analysis  
**Status:** DRAFT (NEEDS-RATIFICATION per Rule 9)

## Hypothesis

We are measuring the J anchor performance and tier diversity (concentration) of the baseline engine (BEARISH_REJECTION_RIDE_THE_RIBBON setup with original parameters) over 16-month SPY 5m data to establish a baseline for future improvements. This validates whether the engine captures J's edge without excessive concentration in a few days.

## Mechanism

The baseline engine trades only the BEARISH_REJECTION_RIDE_THE_RIBBON setup as defined in the playbook, with parameters as of the leaderboard date (e.g., automation/state/params.json). We run a Stage-1 backtest on 16-month SPY 5m data (2025-01-01 to 2026-06-16) and record:
- P&L on each of the 6 J anchor days (3 winners, 3 losers)
- Overall concentration: top-5 days' P&L as % of total P&L
We assume account size sufficient to trade the quantities specified in J anchors ($25K+ per OP-20 disclosure).

## Expected impact on OP-16 anchors

| J day | Current engine behavior | Proposed behavior | Delta |
|---|---|---|---|
| 4/29 winner | +$342 (per playbook example) | +$342 | $0 |
| 5/01 winner | +$470 (per playbook example) | +$470 | $0 |
| 5/04 winner | +$730 (per playbook example) | +$730 | $0 |
| 5/05 loser | $0 (engine skips per OP-16) | $0 (engine skips) | $0 |
| 5/06 loser | $0 (engine skips) | $0 (engine skips) | $0 |
| 5/07 loser 1 | $0 (engine skips) | $0 (engine skips) | $0 |
| 5/07 loser 2 | $0 (engine skips) | $0 (engine skips) | $0 |

(Note: Baseline analysis assumes engine behaves exactly as per playbook examples on J days. Actual backtest may show deviations.)

## OP-20 disclosures

1. **Account-size assumption:** qty=28 requires $25K+; $1K paper ~= 14% headline (based on OP-20 disclosure 1 and playbook position sizing table). For J anchor quantities (max 20 contracts), $25K+ account enables full headline P&L; $1K paper realizes ~14% of headline.
2. **Sample bias:** Sample size = ~336 trading days (16 months). Selection method = entire available 16-month SPY 5m dataset. Overfit risk = low (no parameter optimization; pure performance measurement).
3. **Out-of-sample:** NEEDS-OOS (no IS/OOS split performed in Stage-1 backtest).
4. **Real-fills:** NEEDS-REAL-FILLS (top 3 J days not validated against real OPRA fills).
5. **Failure modes:** 
   - Worst day: Large loss if engine enters losing trade and hits catastrophe stop (e.g., -$500+ on a single day).
   - Max drawdown: Estimated 20-30% based on historical volatility (requires full backtest to quantify).
   - Blow-up scenario: Consecutive loser days exceeding daily loss kill switch (-50% of starting equity).
6. **Concentration:** unknown -- requires Stage-1 backtest (top-5 days % of total P&L not computed without backtest results).

## Pre-merge gate

Gym validators must PASS (81/81 for v41), walk-forward analysis must show OOS/IS Sharpe ratio >= 0.70, and real-fills validation on top 3 J days must show <20% P&L deviation from simulation.

## Confidence

5 / 10 -- Baseline J anchor behavior inferred from playbook examples (3 confirmed winners); actual backtest may reveal deviations due to execution slippage, parameter drift, or unmodeled market conditions. Concentration metric entirely unknown without backtest.

## Pre-existing leaderboard impact

Provides baseline for evaluating all leaderboard candidates. Does not conflict with any existing candidate; complements analysis of gate/filter impacts on J anchors (e.g., ranks 17, 21). Enables quantification of tier diversity changes from proposed improvements.
